question: Unscramble these letters to reveal a famous book and film character: AIMN OPPPRSY
If a name reversed spells a real word — for example, LIAM reversed is MAIL — what do you get if you reverse the name DENNIS?
A coded sequence reads 3S46T89R5E7N68G46T52H9. Strip out all the numbers and you're left with a word inside a well-known three-word saying about continuous improvement. What is the saying?
What replaces the question marks in this sequence: JF3128, MA3130, MJ3130, ??????
Astrid, 32, meets Ed on a date. When she asks his age, he says: 'I'm really the same age as you, plus half my age.' How old is Ed?
The four seasons can each be abbreviated to their first and last letters: SPRING becomes SG, SUMMER becomes SR, AUTUMN becomes AN. What letter completes WINTER's abbreviation, W?
How many animals can you find hidden in this string of letters: PHEASANTORTOISEALLION?
Which two countries need to be swapped so that this list is in the correct order: China, Oman, Russia, Thailand, Ethiopia, Croatia, Rwanda?
answer: Thailand and Rwanda (spelling out C-O-R-R-E-C-T with their first letters)

question: Lily is Pippa's niece, but she isn't Nicola's niece, even though Nicola is Pippa's sister and Pippa has no children. How is this possible?
choices: Nicola is not really her aunt

question: What four-letter word do you get if you follow the directions in these three words: WRIGHT, RECENTER, CLEFT?
answer: CREW (C from cLEFT, RE from the centre of RECENTER, W from wRIGHT)

question: What is the hidden pattern shared by these four phrases: MINOR THERAPY, TEA STRAINER, GLASS OUTHOUSE, FELIXSTOWE STATION?
answer: A compass point (N, S, E, or W) appears across the join of each pair of words
choices: A compass point appears in each

question: JAMAICA + JAPAN share letters in positions that give the number 124. ARGENTINA + ARMENIA gives 1245. FRANCE + BRAZIL gives 23. What number does ENGLAND + GERMANY give?
Manchester has a reputation for being rainy. If it's dry at 12:01am on Monday, what are the chances that it will be sunny exactly 72 hours later, on Thursday?
answer: 0% (12:01am is night-time, so it can't be sunny regardless of weather)

question: You walk 5 miles north, turn east and walk 7 miles, turn south and walk 5 miles, then turn west and walk 7 miles. How many miles away from your starting point are you?
A day lasts approximately 10 hours on Jupiter. If you teleport there from the UK on Monday at 9am and stay for 8 Jupiter days, what day of the week is it in the UK when you teleport back?
When the number 777,777 is written out fully in words, how many times does the letter 'e' appear?
If you write the words HIGHER and LOWER out with their letters alternating (one letter from HIGHER, then one from LOWER, and so on), what do you get?

If you have eight fingers on your left hand, six fingers on your right hand, the same number of toes on your right foot as fingers on your left hand, and three toes on your left foot, how many fingers and toes do you have in total?
In a room of 100 people, 99% are left-handed. How many left-handed people need to leave the room to bring that percentage down to 98%?
Which four-letter word is missing from this list: spot, opts, pots, stop, tops, ____?
Listen carefully: which letter of the alphabet is logically missing from this list — B, C, D, E, G, P, T, Z?
answer: V (all the given letters rhyme with 'ee' when said aloud, but V does not fit that same rhyming group with the missing letter)

question: What word are you left with if you DEBUG this line of code: VANTIFLEACTOBEETLERTICKY?
Logically, which letter comes next in this sequence: S U M O T U W E T H F R S ?
answer: A (each pair of letters is the start of a day of the week: SU, MO, TU, WE, TH, FR, SA)

question: Which letter pair could squeeze into the same position inside each of these words to make longer words: JUICE, PEERING, JEER?
Using the letters in a spoonful of alphabet soup as many times as you like, what is the longest word you could spell?
Last month was not May or July. Next month is not January, March, or November. Neither last month nor next month is April, August, or December. What month must it be now?
Take this jumbled string and remove the words 'two letters' from within it (letter by letter) to reveal a four-word phrase: T I W O A M L A W E T I N T E N R E S R

When Vince got engaged, his best man bought balloons spelling STAG LOCATION. His other friends said he needed more balloons to spell CONGRATULATIONS instead. What three extra letters did he need to buy?
answer: R, U, and N (STAG LOCATION has one N, but CONGRATULATIONS needs two)

question: You should always read the small print carefully. A block of Terms & Conditions is printed with no spaces between forty individual T&Cs. How many errors are hidden in the text?
Yolande takes one tablet every day of the week whose name contains exactly three vowels in total. She starts a pack of 10 tablets on March 2nd. What date will she need to start a new pack?

A travel agent takes bookings: 54 for Lanzarote, 22 for Bali, 34 for Jamaica, 43 for Florida. Following the same rule (replacing a number with a word that starts with the digit it stands for), how many bookings did they take for Ibiza?
What letter completes this sequence, where each letter stands for a unit of time: S M H D W M ?
answer: Y (Seconds, Minutes, Hours, Days, Weeks, Months, Years)

question: VOTING can join the group BANDLEADER, NICKELODEON, and SILVERBACK. What do all four words have hidden in common?
answer: They each contain the name of a metal hidden inside (LEAD, NICKEL, SILVER, and TIN in voTINg)

question: What is the lowest whole number that is equal to ten times the number of letters in its own name when spelled out?
A SPRAINED ANKLE represents an injury. Being told 'YOU ARE STUPID!' represents an insult. What well-known six-word phrase do the two together represent?
What three-letter word can go before SON, FOOD, SICK, BED, and SIDE to make five new words?

Using the same rule throughout — replace the word 'times' with the multiplication symbol X to form words with similar meanings — TE x T equals MESSAGE, and TO x IN equals POISON. What two letters make E x ?? equal LEAVE?
What number replaces the question mark: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 7, 16, 9, ?
answer: 32 (odd positions count up normally 1,3,5,7,9; even positions double each time: 2,4,8,16,32)

question: What is the first number between 1 and 100 whose letters, when spelled out, are in alphabetical order?
answer: FORTY (F, O, R, T, Y are in ascending alphabetical order)

question: If January = 717, March = 5315, and June = 4624 (based on the number of letters in the month's name, its position in the calendar, and the product of those two numbers), what does August equal?
answer: 6848 (August has 6 letters, is the 8th month, and 6 × 8 = 48)

question: Which of these is the correct word for the crossword clue 'Investigate': EXPLORE, EXAMINE, or EXPOSE?

At a zoo, the aardvark is called Adam, the meerkat is called Michelle, and the rhino is called Rochelle — each name starts with the same letter as the animal's name. What is the zebra called: Annabelle or Zachary?
Logically, given this exclusive guest list — Doja Cat, Megan Fox, Sheryl Crow — which of these celebrities also belongs on it: Bad Bunny, Ariana Grande, Post Malone, or Elton John?
answer: Bad Bunny (each name hides an animal: CAT, FOX, CROW, BUNNY)

question: Which name comes next in this sequence, where each surname's initials advance one letter through the alphabet: Ashley Banjo, Charles Dickens, Ella Fitzgerald, ____ ____?
What single word do you get when you combine LEM, ON, and ADE in that order?
What is ABRACADABRA spelled backwards?
All the vowels and spaces have been removed from a headline, leaving LDYGGSNGSGRMMY. The full sentence uses the same single vowel five times. Which vowel is it?
Fork is related by blood to Knife, but not to Spoon. Which two of the three could be identical twins?
Which of these symbols can be drawn without ever lifting your pen from the paper: %, !, &, or =?
answer: & (ampersand; it can be drawn as one continuous stroke)

question: Logically, what name fills the blank in this list: Jay, ____, Elle, Em?
answer: Kay (the names sound like the letters J, K, L, M)

question: GERMANY contains the word MAN, and ARGENTINA contains the word GENT. Which of these countries has the word LAD hidden inside it: Spain, Morocco, or Bangladesh?
A cocktail is one-fifth vodka and 60% lemonade. What percentage of the cocktail is strawberry liqueur?
One election candidate's name is an anagram of the words 'ELECTORAL SYSTEM'. What is the candidate's name?
EVIL, VILE, and VEIL are all anagrams of one another using the same four letters. What other common four-letter word can be made from those same letters?
Susan's minivan seats 2 in front (including the driver), 2 in the middle row, and 3 in the back. How many kids can she safely take in one trip?
If you replaced every vowel in the surname KARDASHIAN with a different vowel, which of these could be the new spelling: KURDUSHAUN, KIRDESHOON, or KORDOSHEEN?

Selena and Camila are going to a 3-floor mall to shop for shoes, eat dinner, and see a movie — each activity is on a different floor. The theater is not on the 1st floor. Neither the 2nd nor 3rd floor has a shoe store. The food court is on the 2nd floor. Which floor is the shoe store on?
Mike, Nell, and Tess take turns counting upward from 1 to 20, one number each, always in that same order (Mike, Nell, Tess, Mike, Nell, Tess...). Who says the number 20?
One of these three famous movie quotes has been misquoted. Which one? A) 'You can't handle the truth!' B) 'May the Force be with you.' C) 'Toto, I've a feeling we're in not Kansas anymore.'
answer: C (it should read 'we're not in Kansas anymore' — the words 'in' and 'not' are swapped)

question: Two animal names can be joined together to spell a longer word that is NOT itself an animal. One of the animals is SEAL — what's the other animal, and what word do they make?
A standard traffic light has green, yellow, and red lights. If the colors were rearranged in alphabetical order from top to bottom, what order would they be in?

A faucet arrived with the words on its handles spelled backwards (a labeling mistake). The plumber who installed it also put the handles on the wrong sides. A second plumber then fixed just one of these two errors. If you turn the handle on the right now, what temperature water comes out?
What animal do you get if you combine the sounds of the words BUN and KNEE?
True or False: It would be False to say that the word 'True' appears more than the word 'False' in this True/False question?
answer: True (the word 'False' actually appears more often, so it IS false that 'True' appears more — making the overall statement True)

question: Four sisters get their hair cut one at a time. Steph goes sometime after Alisha. Holly goes first. Eliza goes sometime after Steph. Which order are they cut in: Holly, Alisha, Steph, Eliza; Alisha, Holly, Eliza, Steph; or Steph, Holly, Alisha, Eliza?
Using a consistent rule, SOFTENING becomes SONG, RADIOACTIVE becomes RAVE, and WHITECOAT becomes WHAT. What four-letter word does FAVOURITE become?
One word in each of these correctly describes where its own capital letter sits: penultimatE, cenTral, iNitial. Which one is actually correct?
answer: cenTral (the capital T is the middle/central letter of the word)

question: A turducken is a turkey stuffed with a duck stuffed with a chicken. Using that same naming logic, what should you call a burger stuffed with a taco stuffed with a pizza?

A bee stung your tongue, so now every 'S' sound you make comes out as 'TH'. Based on the number of 'S' sounds in each word, which of these would give you the most trouble: Senselessness, Success, or Sunglasses?
What kind of storage room do you get by combining the words PAN and TREE?
What common two-word object do you get by combining the concepts of a SAFE, a TEABAG, and a BOWLING PIN?
McHaley's Comet was seen in 2004, then again in 2011, 2018, and 2025 (every 7 years). Brady's Comet appeared in 1983, then 1999, and 2015 (every 16 years). Which comet will be seen next?
answer: Brady's Comet (due in 2031, before McHaley's in 2032)

question: If IYKYK means 'If you know, you know', which of these acronyms would stand for 'If you kayak, you know your kayak'?
An alarm company requires a 10-character password with no repeated characters. Which of these passwords is acceptable: DISARM56$&, PASSWORD12, or SECURE1111?
The first row combines pictures and a letter to make PENDANT. Which single letter completes the word in the second row?
imageAlt: A rebus diagram. The example row shows a pen, the letter D, and an ant, making PENDANT. The question row shows a cat, a blank box, and a log.

question: If the final question of the show was statistically twice as difficult (meaning half as many people could answer it), what would the new name of the show have to be?
ONION, TOMATO, and SALSA all share something in common: each word's first two letters are the same as its last two letters. Which of these also fits: EAGLE, RAZOR, BULB, or ERASER?
What one word in this question uses the greatest number of unique letters that aren't repeated?
What vowel appears exactly four times in total when you write out the names of all seven days of the week?
answer: U (in SUNDAY, TUESDAY, THURSDAY, and SATURDAY)

question: Delete the three letters of a horned farm animal from the word GERANIUMS to leave a common six-letter word. What is it?
What common three-letter word can be made from the three letters of the alphabet that do NOT appear anywhere in this sentence: 'Zeke's mighty quick jambalaya recipe is unviewed'?
answer: FOX (the letters F, O, and X do not appear in the sentence)

question: If a standard traffic light's colors were arranged alphabetically from top to bottom, what would the order be?
Interlock your ten fingers with your left hand on the bottom. Starting with your lowest finger, which finger is sixth?
A wheel has three sections labeled 3 and three sections labeled 4. If you spin it twice, what percentage chance is there that the two spins total 7?
In what year would someone born in 1990 turn the age equal to the sum of the digits in 1990?
How many syllables are there in total when you say the names of all 26 letters of the English alphabet?
Combine the silent letters in INDICTED, HALFWAY, GUARD, AUTUMN, and KNUCKLE, in that order. What sound word do they spell?
Counting down from 100, what is the first number you say whose name is not contained within the name of another number with exactly one syllable?
What is the only month whose name becomes another common English word when spelled backwards?
What animal logically comes next: Aardvark, Rabbit, Raccoon, Haddock, Honeybee, ___?
What word is spelled by the first letters of this clue: FIRST LETTERS OF WORDS EACH REALLY SPELL?
A town named STATE is in a state named COUNTRY, which is in a country named TOWN. Which named place has the largest area?
Someone's favorite instrument is TAMBOURINE, college major is EDUCATION, and favorite building is JAILHOUSE. By the same logic, which is their favorite vegetable?
CUSP, PINTO, QUARTS, ___, GALLEON: which word fills the blank so that removing one letter from each makes a unit of liquid measurement?
Zora's birthday is the second-to-last day of the year and Lana's is the second day of the year. If today is Zora's birthday, how many days until Lana's?
If A = 25, B = 24, C = 23, and so on, what do you get when you multiply the values of all the letters in MEGAHERTZ?
Amrit and his grandad share the same birthday. Their birthday balloons arrive muddled up. Amrit's grandad is three times older than him. How old is Amrit?
imageAlt: Four glossy gold foil number balloons floating in a row on a cream background, reading the digits 6, 8, 7, 2.

question: Which number replaces the question mark in this grid?
imageAlt: A 4 column by 6 row grid. Row 2 column 2 holds 2253. Row 3 column 4 holds 4341. Row 6 column 1 holds a highlighted question mark. Row 6 column 4 holds 4611.

question: If both dice keep rolling the way the arrows show, what will the two top faces total at the end?
imageAlt: A checkerboard with two dice. Die A follows a longer curved arrow to a highlighted tile and lands on 4. Die B follows a shorter curved arrow to a highlighted tile and lands on 1.

question: The two digits below read 24, made of matchsticks. Move exactly ONE matchstick to a different position to create a new 3-digit number. What is that number?
imageAlt: A digit 2 made of five matchsticks and an open-style digit 4 made of three matchsticks, laid out on a dark background, together reading 24.

question: In the diagram, LLL = A and RRR = H. What word is represented by the path LLR LLL LRR RRL RLL?
imageAlt: A binary tree branching left or right three times from START down to eight leaves labeled A through H.

question: What word is represented by this picture?
imageAlt: A single semi-transparent teal glass cube drawn in 3D isometric style, with a large bold numeral 8 on its front-right face.

question: Each box below names the fill colour of the NEXT box in the loop. What word replaces the question mark?
imageAlt: Eight colored boxes arranged in a loop, each box's word naming the fill colour of the next box in the chain.

question: How many squares make up this pattern?
imageAlt: Four identical square outlines sharing a centre point, each rotated further than the last, forming a 16-pointed star.

question: Four people are in a maze trying to reach the centre. Which of them is going the wrong way?
imageAlt: A circular maze backdrop with four color-coded dotted routes. The routes for A, C, and D each wind inward and connect all the way to the highlighted GOAL circle at the centre. The route for B winds inward but stops at a solid dead-end wall, labeled, well short of the centre.

question: Look at this floor covered in square tiles. If each square tile is made of eight triangles, how many square tiles were used to cover the floor?
imageAlt: A grid of pinwheel-patterned floor tiles, four tiles wide by five tiles tall, each tile made of eight small triangles.

question: What phrase is represented here? (You don't need football knowledge to work it out.)
answer: ALL FOR ONE AND ONE FOR ALL
choices: ALL FOR ONE AND ONE FOR ALL
ONE FOR ALL AND ALL FOR ONE
UNITED WE STAND DIVIDED WE FALL
imageAlt: A box of four football scorelines all reading 4 dash 1, the word AND, and a second box with one scoreline reading 4 dash 4.

question: Which of these words does NOT make a real word when its letters are read in reverse order: STUN, DUST, or SNUB?
answer: DUST (reversed it spells TSUD, which is not a word; STUN reversed is NUTS and SNUB reversed is BUNS)

question: Which of these is the 'largest': CIRCLE, TRIANGLE, RECTANGLE, or SQUARE?
answer: RECTANGLE (it has the most letters of the four words — 9 — making it the 'largest' word)

question: If you swap the first letter of the word for the next letter of the alphabet, which of these becomes a valid new word: PAGE, RAGE, or WAGE?
answer: RAGE (becomes SAGE, a valid word; PAGE would become QAGE and WAGE would become XAGE, neither of which are real words)

question: What single term is both a Marvel superhero and a triathlon event?
Which two letters are missing to complete this musical instrument: _K_L_L_?

You set your alarm clock 5 minutes ahead so you'll arrive 5 minutes early for a meeting the next day. You forgot the clocks spring forward an hour overnight for daylight saving time. How will you actually arrive for the meeting?
answer: 55 min late (you set your clock 5 minutes early, but the clocks also sprang forward an hour overnight, so you actually arrive 55 minutes after the meeting starts)

question: Given Miss Tep, Miss Poke, and Miss Ing, who logically is the fourth woman: Miss Robb, Miss Lyle, Miss Wells, or Miss Pell?
answer: Miss Pell (each name is 'Miss' plus a suffix that forms a word when read together: Miss+Tep=misstep, Miss+Poke=mispoke, Miss+Ing=missing, Miss+Pell=misspell)

question: What word can be put after each of these three words to make three new words: SAUSAGE, DRUM, SPRING?
answer: Roll (sausage roll, drum roll, spring roll)

question: What single word means all three of these things: a loud noise, a piece of tennis equipment, and a fraudulent scheme?
What item of clothing can you add after the letters 'A D' to make a commonly used word?
Which of these five rows of six symbols reads exactly the same left-to-right as it does right-to-left?
imageAlt: Five labeled rows of six symbols each. Row A: bracket close, bracket open, repeated. Row B: exclamation, pound sign, repeated. Row C: less-than, greater-than, greater-than, greater-than, greater-than, less-than. Row D: percent, slash, repeated. Row E: plus, plus, minus, minus, minus, plus. Only row C is a palindrome.

question: Rows show eight diamonds, six spades, and six hearts. Following the same hidden logic, how many club symbols belong in the fourth row?
imageAlt: Four rows of card suit symbols with labels: eight red diamonds, six black spades, six red hearts, and a row of clubs marked with a question mark. The count of symbols in each row equals the number of letters in that suit's name.

question: On a seven-segment digital display, how many digits from 0 to 9 light exactly as many segments as their own numeric value?
imageAlt: Ten seven-segment display digits, 0 through 9, in green on a dark panel with unlit segments shown dimly. Digit 4 lights four segments, digit 5 lights five segments, and digit 6 lights six segments; no other digit's lit segment count equals its own value.

question: A thick bar covers the middle of a diagonal line. Which of the three lines on the other side (A, B, or C) is the true unbroken continuation?
imageAlt: A thin diagonal line rises toward a thick vertical bar. On the other side of the bar, three labeled diagonal lines continue at the same angle but at different heights; only line A truly aligns with the original line's trajectory.

question: A Venn diagram shows three overlapping circles: PALINDROME, FOUR LETTERS, and ENDS IN P. Which word belongs in the very centre, where all three circles overlap: STOP, BRING, LEVEL, or PEEP?
answer: PEEP (it is a palindrome, has exactly four letters, and ends in P, satisfying all three circles at once)
imageAlt: A three-circle Venn diagram labeled PALINDROME, ENDS IN P, and FOUR LETTERS. LEVEL sits only in PALINDROME. STOP sits where ENDS IN P and FOUR LETTERS overlap. BRING sits outside all three circles. PEEP sits in the centre where all three circles overlap.

question: Which four-letter word completes all three of these: dress ____, Green Cross ____, and bar____?
The letters of a common food have been sorted into alphabetical order to spell ABDER. What is the food?
In which of these words does every single letter appear more than once, with none appearing only once: NONSENSE, MURMUR, or ENDANGERED?
answer: MURMUR (every letter M, U, and R appears exactly twice; NONSENSE has a lone O and ENDANGERED has a lone A, G, and R)

question: Which of these names does NOT contain a hidden time of day: Carl Noonan, Dan Thomas, Meg Nightingale, or Becky Morningside?
answer: Dan Thomas (the others hide NOON, NIGHT, and MORNING)

question: Which single letter, added to CONTACT, makes a word meaning 'to become smaller'?
In a coding system, even numbers are colored blue and odd numbers are colored red. If you add two red numbers together, what color represents the result?
answer: Blue (odd plus odd always equals an even number)

question: How many different letters appear more than once in the phrase 'ONE PERCENT CLUB'?
If GOAL minus the letters A and L, plus the letters L and F, spells a sport, what is it?
May 5, 2025 (5/5/25) was a 'square root day' — the month and day are equal, and multiplying them gives the last two digits of the year (5x5=25). In which month did the previous square root day fall?

UK coins ordered from smallest to largest by physical size are: 5p, 1p, 20p, old round GBP1, 10p, 2p, 50p, GBP2. If instead ordered from lowest to highest by value, how many coins stay in the exact same position?
answer: 1 (only the GBP2 coin, since it's both the largest and the highest-value)

question: Adding the same single vowel to each of these words makes a new word: BY, FUR, LOG, PEN. Which vowel is it?
Which single word can mean the same as all three of these: FINAL, ENDURE, and PREVIOUS?
OWN means 'possess' and NOW means 'immediately' — both are anagrams of the same three letters. Which word, also an anagram of those same letters, means 'victorious' (the past tense of 'win')?
Jim is 6'3" tall. Erica stands on a 1-foot stool and becomes 2 inches taller than Jim. How tall is Erica really?
Which three different positive whole numbers give you the exact same total whether you add them together or multiply them together?
There's a nine-letter word: remove its first five letters and 'some' remains; remove its last four letters and it becomes 'whole'. What is the word?
What number replaces the question mark in this sequence, where each number is the letter-count of a month's name in calendar order: 7, 8, 5, 5, 3, 4, 4, ?, 9, 7, 8, 8
What weather-related phrase do these four royal titles represent: Emperor Tiger, King Tabby, Queen Spaniel, Empress Labrador?
answer: Raining cats and dogs (royalty 'reigns', a homophone of 'rains'; Tiger and Tabby are cats, Spaniel and Labrador are dogs)

question: 2025's digits add up to 9 (2+0+2+5). What is the NEXT year whose digits also add up to 9?
Change the first letter of DOG, PAIN, FIST, and MAIL to make four weather-related words. Which weather word do you get from PAIN?
Which everyday saying is being described by this alliterative riddle: 'Illustrious intellects imagine identically'?
choices: Two heads are better than one
Birds of a feather flock together

question: What two-letter combination can go before APED, INK, ONE, OWNED, and OUGHT to make five new words?
Roll three standard dice. If their face values must add up to exactly 10, what is the highest 3-digit number you could form by arranging them in the best order?
answer: 631 (6, 3, 1 — no other combination summing to 10 with faces 1-6 arranges to a bigger number)

question: The sum of the first 10 odd numbers (1+3+5+...+19) is 100. What is the sum of the first 10 even numbers (2+4+6+...+20)?
If you sort all 52 playing cards (no jokers) alphabetically by their full name, e.g. 'Ace of Clubs', which card comes last?
answer: Two of Spades (rank names sort with 'Two' last alphabetically, and among the four 'Two of ___' cards, 'Spades' sorts last)
